## Methods & notes

This single-sample multi-omics report layers ADT, VDJ, and ATAC analyses on top
of an annotated ScSeurat object (or a BD Rhapsody pre-built Seurat). The RNA
assay stays the default so the existing exploreSC Shiny app renders the RNA
UMAP unchanged.

- **ADT**: `processADT()` builds a Seurat v5 ADT assay, normalizes (ADTnorm or
  CLR), runs PCA on antibody features, computes `adt.umap`. Top markers in
  the **ADT** tab are ranked by mean normalized signal.
- **VDJ**: `processVDJ()` wraps `scRepertoire::combineTCR/BCR` and
  `combineExpression`. Cells receive `CTaa`, `CTgene`, `cloneSize`, and
  `clonalFrequency` metadata. Repertoire statistics, V/J usage, and
  per-cluster occupancy are computed via scRepertoire 2.x.
- **ATAC**: `processATAC()` builds a `Signac::ChromatinAssay` from the
  CellRanger ARC peak matrix, runs TF-IDF + SVD + UMAP, and adds a
  `GeneActivity` assay when an EnsDb annotation is available for the genome.
- **WNN**: `runWNN()` runs `FindMultiModalNeighbors` on the available
  dimensional reductions (`pca`, `adt.pca`, `lsi`) when at least two are
  present. Per-modality weights are stored as `*.weight` columns.
- **BD Rhapsody**: `loadBDRhapsody()` reads the per-sample `*_Seurat.rds`
  shipped by the BDRhapsodySA app, prefixes barcodes `<Sample>_<barcode>`,
  computes a fresh RNA PCA so WNN can run, and reuses the RNA + ADT assays
  already built upstream.
